goal: AGENTS.md v5: instruct agent to also READ team intents (mainline log + show + list-proposals) for context, not just write them. Intent metadata (why/decisions/risks) is richer than code alone.

summary.title: AGENTS.md v5 — agent contract is read+write, not just write
summary.what: Bumps the AGENTS.md template to v5 with a new 'Read team intents for context (do this aggressively)' section naming three commands: mainline log, mainline show <id>, mainline list-proposals. Reframes the opening paragraph so agents know they're expected to consume team intents, not just produce their own. Same content lands in the IDE stub template.
summary.why: v4 covered start/append/seal well but the read side was a single under-emphasised line. Intent metadata (why, decisions, risks, followups) is strictly richer than the diff; agents that don't read intents are working with less context than they could.
summary.user_goal: Make agents look at recent team intent history before designing changes.

decisions.point: Where to place the read section
decisions.chose: Between 'At the start of a task' and 'While working'
decisions.rationale: It's an orientation step; comes after status/start (which establish your own intent) and before the actual work.

decisions.chose: log + show + list-proposals as the canonical trio
decisions.rationale: log is the index, show is the detail view, list-proposals is the in-flight check; covers historical (merged) and current (proposed) without bloat.

risks: Adding read steps adds CLI calls per task; budget cost is acceptable but could be over-used.
followups: A `mainline search <keyword>` command would beat manual log+grep+show; consider for v0.3.

architectural_claims: The agent contract is symmetric: read team intents + write own intent.
behavioral_changes: AGENTS.md and IDE stubs include a 'Read team intents for context' section.
migration_notes: Existing AGENTS.md from v4 gets the v5 content automatically via the section-aware upsert from PR #25.
